summary:
The term "malignant pustule" describes cutaneous anthrax characterized by an ulcerated skin lesion with a central black eschar caused by toxin-producing Bacillus anthracis bacteria. It corresponds well with option B based on these defining features. \boxed{B}
To summarize Agent1’s reasoning into two sentences focusing only on key clinical points for clarity regarding what “malignant pustule” means clinically:

Malignant pustule denotes cutaneous anthrax featuring a characteristic ulcer with a central black eschar from bacterial toxins. Clinically, it uniquely identifies lesions produced by \(Bacillus\) species like those seen in anthrax infections. 

This summary captures essential elements while adhering strictly within the specified length constraint of just under fifty words focused purely on core medical information pertinent to identifying this condition accurately among options provided or generally understood contextually without extraneous details.
